column1: CP - Feedback and next steps
column32:
*As a* Business Analyst or System Architect,
*I want to* conduct a comprehensive investigation and business discovery of the Forth Cordoba portal,
*so that* I can accurately map the business logic, document critical user journeys, and ensure the CRM alignment with our core business requirements.

h3. Acceptance Criteria Checklist (Forth Cordoba Portal)

* -*Portal Mapping:* All key modules, data entities, and user roles within the Forth Cordoba portal are identified and documented.-
* -*Logic Documentation:* A clear definition of the "source of truth" for business rules is established, including data validation rules and status transitions.-
* -*E2E Validation:* Successful execution of at least one full business cycle (entry to resolution) is completed and the data flow is mapped.-
* -*Constraint Identification:* Mandatory constraints for successful transactions and automated triggers are explicitly defined.-
* -*Gap Analysis:* A list of clarifying questions regarding ambiguous system behaviors or business rules is compiled for stakeholder review.-
* -*UX Flow Review:* A typical business user's daily interaction flow is tested.-



h3. Acceptance Criteria Checklist (Our Cordoba Portal)

* *Portal Mapping:* All key modules, data entities, and user roles within the Forth Cordoba portal are identified and documented.
* *Logic Documentation:* A clear definition of the "source of truth" for business rules is established, including data validation rules and status transitions.
* *E2E Validation:* Successful execution of at least one full business cycle (entry to resolution) is completed and the data flow is mapped.
* *Constraint Identification:* Mandatory constraints for successful transactions and automated triggers are explicitly defined.
* *Gap Analysis:* A list of clarifying questions regarding ambiguous system behaviors or business rules is compiled for stakeholder review.
* *UX Flow Review:* A typical business user's daily interaction flow is tested.

column1: CX - Perform testing on a first login button with Nicolas.
column32:
*Description*
Work with Diana to review the CreditorX iOS application on a personal iPhone and investigate the issue where the *"Is your first login?"* button is not working on the login screen.

The goal is to properly configure the iPhone and application setup, validate whether the issue can be reproduced following the scenarios reported by users, and identify the root cause if possible.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -iPhone configured to run the CreditorX application correctly-
* -Login flow reviewed on a physical device-
* -"Is your first login?" issue reproduced or ruled out-
* -Reported user scenarios tested and validated-
* -Findings documented and shared with the team-
* Root cause identified if sufficient evidence is available

*Deliverables*

h3. *Findings*

* Some configuration issues were encountered while setting up and running the CreditorX application on the physical device, mainly related to Xcode certificates and signing configuration.
* After resolving the setup issues, the reported *“Is your first login?”* problem could not be reproduced on an *iPhone 14 Pro*, as the view and navigation worked correctly on this device.
* However, an additional UI issue was identified in the first screen of the registration flow. When the device is using dark mode, one of the components appears to behave like an editable input. Tapping the text opens the keyboard and allows the content to be modified, as shown in the attached video.

[^ScreenRecording_07-22-2026 14-34-14_1.mov]

[^ScreenRecording_07-22-2026 14-35-22_1.mov]

column1: CP - Implement ErrorKey for invalid current password in Change Password endpoint
column32:
*Description*
Update the *Change Password* endpoint to return an {{ErrorKey}} when the user enters an incorrect current password. The endpoint currently returns only a status object without an {{ErrorKey}}, making it inconsistent with the application's standardized error response format and preventing client applications from displaying the appropriate localized error message.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -Change Password endpoint returns an {{ErrorKey}} for an invalid current password-
* -Error response follows the application's standard error contract-
* -Existing success response remains unchanged-

*Deliverables*

column32:

Implement the generation, persistence, and transmission of a stable device identifier ({{X-Device-Id}}) in Cordoba Portal Android. * -Device identifier persisted across application sessions-
* -OTP challenge skipped for trusted devices according to backend rules-
* -Implementation validated across login and profile MFA-

*Deliverables*

column1: CP - WEB/IOS - Password change: "Incorrect Current Password" error not localized
column32:
*Description*

When changing the password in Profile and the entered current password is incorrect, the error message is shown in English ("Incorrect Current Password" / "Current password is incorrect") regardless of the app's active language, instead of showing the corresponding translated message. Affects both the web portal and the iOS app.

*Steps to reproduce*: Profile → change password → enter an incorrect current password → Continue (with the app set to Spanish).

!image (5).png|width=239,alt="image (5).png"!

!image (6).png|width=237,alt="image (6).png"!

*Acceptance Criteria*

* Web: the error message is shown in the correct language according to the app's active locale, matching the translation already defined in the dictionaries.
* iOS: the error message is shown in the correct language according to the app's active locale, matching the translation already defined in {{Localizable.xcstrings}}.
* Verified with the app set to both English and Spanish, on both platforms.

*Deliverables*

column1: Spike: Pulse vs XNLogger — Network Debugging Tool for Cordoba Portal iOS
column32:
*Description*

Cordoba Portal iOS has no in-app tool to inspect network requests/responses (REST via Moya, GraphQL via Apollo), unlike creditorx-ios (already uses XNLogger) and both Android apps (native Android Studio Network Inspector). This spike investigates two candidates — *XNLogger* and *Pulse* — to decide which one to adopt for Cordoba Portal.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -Comparison table of XNLogger vs Pulse: coverage (Moya/Apollo/WebSocket), console UX, log export/sharing, remote viewing, sensitive-data handling, integration effort, maintenance/license.-
* -Confirm chosen tool works with existing build-config gating (Debug/Local/Staging vs Release).-
* -Final recommendation with rationale — no implementation in this task.-

*Deliverables*

column1: CP - Review Terms of Service and Privacy Policy
column32:
*Description*

The onboarding "agreement" screen ({{ob-agreement}}, "Read Agreement") displays Terms of Service / Privacy Policy text that predates the current team taking over the project. This text is incomplete, with several placeholder fields never filled in. A more complete version of both documents was found in recovered project documentation, but those documents also have unfilled sections, and it hasn't been confirmed whether they are the approved, up-to-date source of truth.

Additionally, none of the three clients (web, iOS, Android) have a dedicated section where users can view these documents outside of the onboarding flow — it's the only place this content currently surfaces.

This needs to be clarified with the project stakeholder before any content or UI changes are made, since it's legal content shown directly to users.

[https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CL/pages/185926481/Terms+of+Service+and+Privacy+Policy|https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CL/pages/185926481/Terms+of+Service+and+Privacy+Policy|smart-link] 

[^CP - Privacy Policy (4.20.25)(60991092.2).doc] 

[^CP - Terms of Service (4.20.25)(60994211.2).doc] 

!CP - WEB - Agreement -  2026-07-15 at 11.58.02 AM.mov|width=320,alt="CP - WEB - Agreement -  2026-07-15 at 11.58.02 AM.mov"!

*Acceptance Criteria*

* Confirm with the stakeholder whether the recovered documents are the real, approved source of truth (or obtain the correct current version).
* Define whether Terms of Service and Privacy Policy should have their own dedicated section in the apps, separate from onboarding.
* Define what content should populate the onboarding "agreement" screen going forward (completing the current text vs. replacing it with the approved documents).
* Document the decision so it can be used to plan the implementation work.

*Deliverables*

column1: CP - Configure dev.cordobalegal.com and activate full dev environment
column32:
*Description*

The dev environment cluster is fully deployed and healthy but {{dev.cordobalegal.com}} is not publicly accessible because the domain's DNS is still managed by GoDaddy instead of Route53. The Route53 hosted zone already has all the necessary records configured.

*Acceptance Criteria*

column1: CP - Implement Google Analytics in Login section
column32:
*Description*
Implement Google Analytics tracking in the Login section to begin evaluating how GA4 behaves within Cordoba Portal. The goal is to understand what metrics and events can be collected, explore the available analytics capabilities, and learn how to configure and use the GA4 admin panel.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* Google Analytics integrated in the Login section
* Login-related events successfully tracked in GA4
* Initial metrics and event behavior reviewed and documented
* GA4 admin configuration explored and validated
* Findings and potential next steps documented for future analytics implementation

*Deliverables*
